compiled_state.title: inbound-bridge: git push to tickets branch rejected (fetch first) -> workflow fails when tickets branch updated concurrently
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
The Inbound Bridge GitHub Actions workflow fails at step 'Commit CREATE events back to tickets branch' with: `! [rejected] HEAD -> tickets (fetch first)`. The workflow checks out the tickets branch, commits new CREATE events from Jira, then pushes. If the tickets branch was updated between checkout and push (by the Outbound Bridge or a local merge-to-main.sh), the push is rejected as non-fast-forward.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Inbound Bridge runs on schedule
2. Between its checkout and push, another process pushes to the tickets branch
3. Push fails with 'fetch first' rejection

## Expected Behavior
The Inbound Bridge should pull/rebase before pushing, or retry on push rejection, similar to how merge-to-main.sh handles the tickets branch.

## Actual Behavior
Non-fast-forward push fails and the workflow exits with code 1.

## Evidence
3 failures in the last week — all identical:
- Run 24313779929 (2026-04-12T18:46)
- Run 24286971907 (2026-04-11T16:45)
- Run 24264426464 (2026-04-10T21:12)
